behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed; unchanged files are skipped.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between PR-change collection and file-analysis orchestration (MOCK-MISS). A pure unit test of filtering logic can miss wiring bugs like wrong path normalization or incorrect merge of changed-file lists. No real OS/network/subprocess dependency is required for the core check, so System is not the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Create a repo fixture with changed and unchanged files, feed a realistic PR changed-file list into the pipeline entrypoint, run analysis, and assert analyzers are invoked only for changed paths and never for unchanged ones.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on interaction between rule outputs, severity mapping, grouping, and comment rendering (MOCK-MISS). Unit-testing only the grouping helper may miss integration mismatches (e.g., severity enum translation). Real GitHub API posting is not required to catch this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline comment-generation path with mixed-severity findings from multiple rules and assert the generated PR comment payload has distinct error/warning/info sections with correct membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request touching 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute GitHub Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: The requirement is explicitly tied to CI runtime limits and runner behavior (REAL-INFRA). Local module-only tests do not reliably expose timeout risk; mocking runtime/IO can hide real bottlenecks (MOCK-HIDE).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step in a GitHub Actions-equivalent job (or actual Actions) against a synthetic 200-file PR fixture, measure end-to-end wall-clock duration, and assert completion under 10 minutes including comment generation.

behaviors.description: If the linter process crashes on a malformed file, remaining files are still analyzed and results are posted.
behaviors.justification: The behavior centers on real process-crash isolation and recovery (REAL-INFRA). Mocking the linter as a normal exception can miss true subprocess crash semantics (exit codes/signals, partial output), so System is the minimum.
behaviors.test_description: Run the orchestrator with a real linter subprocess and include one malformed file that triggers a hard crash; assert the pipeline continues analyzing subsequent files and emits a final aggregated PR comment/results artifact.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in output.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily deterministic merge/dedup logic on findings data within one local boundary. No real infra or subprocess is needed, and the specific failure can be caught directly by exercising the dedup function with overlapping-rule cases.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedup component with findings containing same-file/same-line overlaps from different rules and assert only one output finding per dedup key while preserving expected severity/message policy.
